title: NQ Replenished Bid Launchpad Into Upper Liquidity
focus_questions: What evidence justifies upgrading this level into strong support?
Did the defended area remain valid after the launch?

summary.thesis: Repeated same-price bid replenishment that survives pressure can become a true launchpad toward upper liquidity.
summary.operator_translation: A visible bid is not enough; the level must reload, hold, and produce real upward response.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prioritize this card when replay data shows same-price defense, nearby focus support, and room toward upper liquidity.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: defended bid zone under repeated pressure with room to travel higher. Required evidence: same-price replenishment, no clean lower acceptance, and upward reclaim or initiative. Do not trade: on one visible bid or when replenishment stops. Real trigger: the defended price survives multiple hits and price starts reclaiming from it. Failure point: acceptance below the defended zone or no travel toward the upper target.

when_relevant: The same bid price or a narrow zone is replenished repeatedly.
Price cannot continue lower despite repeated pressure.
There is still an upper liquidity target worth launching toward.
required_evidence: same_price_replenishment is observed across multiple tests
significant_liquidity remains active near the defended zone
price stops making clean lower lows and begins reclaiming structure
confirmation_signals: the same price gets reloaded across multiple tests
price begins reclaiming from the defended area
an upper target remains open and price starts traveling toward it
invalidation_signals: replenishment stops and price begins accepting below the defended zone
buyers cannot reclaim structure after the defense
the upper target is too compromised or immediately rejects the move
no_trade_conditions: Do not buy because one large bid flashes on the DOM without repeated same-price replenishment.
Do not buy if replenishment stops and price starts accepting below the defended zone.
Do not call the zone strong support when the bigger context is still pushing directly into a stronger opposing region above.

entry_archetypes.name: reclaim after defense
entry_archetypes.description: Long after the defended bid survives multiple hits and price reclaims the nearest micro pivot.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim runs straight into heavy overhead pressure without room.

entry_archetypes.name: controlled retest of defended price
entry_archetypes.description: Long on a retest of the defended area once renewed buyer response is visible.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the retest becomes deep acceptance below the defended level.

management_notes: Upgrade the level only after repeated defense plus real response, not on visibility alone.
Track whether the defended area still matters after the first launch.
If the first upper target is harvested, hand the script off to post-harvest logic.
review_questions: What specific evidence justified upgrading the level from visible support to strong defended support?
Did the entry wait for reclaimed structure, or was it placed too early against active sell pressure?
After the launch, did the defended area remain valid on revisit or fail immediately?
